vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on cron.monthly
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/cron.monthly, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.monthly
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then find -H /etc/cron.monthly/ -maxdepth 0 -perm /u+s,g+xwrs,o+xwrt -type d -exec chmod u-s,g-xwrs,o-xwrt {} \; else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Setting the correct permissions on /etc/cron.monthly ensures that only privileged users can read or modify the cron configuration, reducing the risk of unauthorized changes that could lead to privilege escalation or service disruption. This aligns with STIG hardening guidelines for file permissions.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Incorrect permissions could allow non‑privileged users to modify cron jobs, leading to privilege escalation.; Overly restrictive permissions could break legitimate cron jobs, causing service availability issues.
